title: Toroloom — Broker Performance & Auth
description: Dedicated monitoring for broker connection health, authentication failures, failover events, rate limiting activity, and tick dispatch latency across all broker backends (Zerodha, Angel, Mock).

panels.title: Broker Status
panels.description: Real-time broker connection health, authentication error rates, and failover activity across all configured broker backends.

panels.title: Overall Broker Health
panels.description: Aggregate broker connection status. 1 = at least one broker is connected, 0 = all brokers are disconnected (critical).

panels.title: Connected Brokers
panels.description: Count of brokers currently reporting connected (1) status. The chain has 3 backends: zerodha, angel, mock.

panels.title: Auth Errors / min
panels.description: Running rate of broker authentication failures across all backends. Sustained values above 1/min indicate credential issues or API outages.

panels.title: Reconnects / min
panels.description: Running rate of broker reconnection or failover events. Any sustained value above 0 indicates network instability or broker API downtime.

panels.title: Broker Connection Details
panels.description: Per-broker connection state, authentication failure distribution, and failover events broken down by backend.

panels.title: Broker Connection State
panels.description: Connection status per broker over time. A drop to 0 triggers automatic failover down the chain (Zerodha → Angel → Mock). Bars indicate state changes clearly.

panels.title: Auth Errors by Broker
panels.description: Authentication error rate per broker over time. Spikes in zerodha or angel indicate upstream API token expiry or credential issues. Mock broker should never have auth errors.

panels.title: Reconnects by Broker
panels.description: Reconnection / failover rate per broker. Zerodha reconnects indicate upstream API issues. Mock reconnects indicate internal fallback activation.

panels.title: Active Broker Count Over Time
panels.description: Number of brokers actively connected. Drops below 3 indicate failover activity. A drop to 0 is a critical outage.

panels.title: Auth & Rate Limiting
panels.description: Authentication health metrics and WebSocket rate limiter activity. Sustained rate limiting indicates aggressive clients or potential DoS.

panels.title: Authentication Error Rate
panels.description: Total auth error rate across all brokers with trend analysis. The 5-minute and 15-minute moving averages help distinguish transient spikes from sustained issues.

panels.title: Rate Limiter Activity
panels.description: WebSocket rate-limited messages per minute (red) vs active rate limiter connections (blue). Sustained rate limiting above 0 indicates aggressive clients; growing active limiters suggest a DoS pattern.

panels.title: Peak Auth Error Rate (1h)
panels.description: Highest auth error rate observed in the last hour. Helps identify worst-case auth failure intensity.

panels.title: Peak Reconnect Rate (1h)
panels.description: Highest reconnect rate observed in the last hour. Helps identify worst-case failover storm intensity.

panels.title: Max Active Rate Limiters (1h)
panels.description: Highest number of connections simultaneously in rate-limited state over the last hour. Values above 0 indicate the rate limiter is actively throttling clients.

panels.title: Total Rate Limited Today
panels.description: Cumulative count of all rate-limited WebSocket messages since midnight. Helps track daily rate limiting volume.

panels.title: Broker Performance (Tick Dispatch)
panels.description: Tick dispatch latency by broker and user, showing how quickly price ticks are marshalled and sent to connected clients.

panels.title: Tick Dispatch Latency (p50 / p95 / p99)
panels.description: Percentile breakdown of tick dispatch latency. The p50-p95 gap reveals outlier ticks causing latency spikes. If p99 is 10x p95, individual large payloads (many symbols) are the bottleneck. Values above 1ms warrant investigation.

panels.title: Current p99 Latency
panels.description: Current 99th percentile tick dispatch latency. Values above 1ms indicate backpressure or serialization bottlenecks.

panels.title: Broker Performance Score
panels.description: Composite health score (0–100) based on broker connected status, auth error rate, reconnect rate, and tick dispatch p99 latency. 100 = all brokers healthy and performing well.

targets.expr:
(
  max(toroloom_broker_connected) * 40
  +
  clamp_max((1 - sum(rate(toroloom_broker_auth_errors_total[5m])) / 5), 1) * 25
  +
  clamp_max((1 - sum(rate(toroloom_broker_reconnects_total[5m])) / 3), 1) * 15
  +
  clamp_max((1 - histogram_quantile(0.99, sum(rate(toroloom_broker_tick_dispatch_seconds_bucket[5m])) by (le)) / 0.01), 1) * 20
)

panels.title: Broker Health Tables
panels.description: Detailed breakdowns of authentication errors, reconnection events, and tick dispatch latency by broker and user.

panels.title: Auth Errors by Broker (Total)
panels.description: Cumulative authentication errors per broker. A growing count for zerodha or angel indicates credential issues. Mock broker errors indicate internal fallback chain problems.

panels.title: Reconnects by Broker (Total)
panels.description: Cumulative reconnection/failover events per broker. Higher counts for zerodha indicate upstream API instability. Mock reconnects indicate the fallback was activated.

panels.title: Broker Snapshot
panels.description: Live snapshot of all broker metrics — connection status, auth errors, and reconnects for each backend at a glance.

panels.title: Top Users by Tick Activity
panels.description: Users receiving the most price ticks and those with the highest tick dispatch latency — useful for identifying high-traffic clients and potential serialization bottlenecks.

panels.title: Top Users by Tick Rate
panels.description: Users with the highest tick delivery rate. Users receiving the most ticks per second are the heaviest consumers of broker data.

panels.title: Top Users by Latency
panels.description: Users with the highest current p99 tick dispatch latency. High latency for specific users may indicate slow network connections or large symbol watchlists.

panels.title: Raw Metrics
panels.description: Raw Prometheus values for debugging broker and auth metrics.

panels.title: Raw Broker Metrics Sampler
panels.description: Raw Prometheus values for all toroloom_broker_* and toroloom_ws_rate_* metrics. Useful for ad-hoc debugging.
